Title: Successful user login with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- User account exists in the database with valid email and password.
- Client application is open and connected to the server.
Test Steps:
1. User enters `testuser@example.com` in the email field.
2. User enters `SecurePass123!` in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- Server retrieves user data from the database.
- Server validates credentials and returns success.
- Client receives user data and grants access to the system.

Title: Failed login with invalid password
Preconditions:
- User account exists in the database with valid email but incorrect password.
- Client application is open and connected to the server.
Test Steps:
1. User enters `testuser@example.com` in the email field.
2. User enters `WrongPass123!` in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- Server retrieves user data from the database.
- Server validates credentials and returns a "login failed" error.
- Client displays an error message: "Invalid email or password."

Title: Failed login with non-existent email
Preconditions:
- No user account exists with the provided email.
- Client application is open and connected to the server.
Test Steps:
1. User enters `nonexistent@example.com` in the email field.
2. User enters `SecurePass123!` in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- Server queries the database and finds no matching user.
- Server returns a "login failed" error.
- Client displays an error message: "Invalid email or password."

Title: User logout
Preconditions: - User is logged in to the system.
Expected Result:
- Server processes the logout request.
- User session is terminated.
- Client redirects to the login screen or home page.

Title: Create a new user account
Preconditions:
- Client application is open and connected to the server.
- No existing account with the provided email.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Sign Up" button.
2. User fills in the registration form with the provided data.
3. User clicks the "Create Account" button.
Expected Result:
- Server validates input fields (non-empty, valid email format).
- Server creates a new user record in the database.
- Server returns success confirmation.
- Client displays a success message: "Account created successfully."

Title: Create a user account with invalid email format
Preconditions: - Client application is open and connected to the server.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Sign Up" button.
2. User fills in the registration form with the provided data.
3. User clicks the "Create Account" button.
Expected Result:
- Server detects invalid email format.
- Server returns an error message: "Please enter a valid email address."
- Client highlights the email field in red and prevents account creation.

Title: Update user profile (name and surname)
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- User profile exists with initial name and surname.
Test Data:
- Current Name: `John`
- Current Surname: `Doe`
- New Name: `Jonathan`
- New Surname: `Doe-Smith`
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Profile" section.
2. User clicks the "Edit" button.
3. User updates the name to `Jonathan` and surname to `Doe-Smith`.
4. User clicks the "Save" button.
Expected Result:
- Server validates the new name and surname (non-empty).
- Server updates the user record in the database.
- Client displays a success message: "Profile updated successfully."

Title: Set user as a seller
Preconditions: - User is logged in and not currently a seller (`isVenditore = false`).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Profile" section.
2. User toggles the "Become a Seller" switch to `true`.
3. User clicks the "Save" button.
Expected Result:
- Server updates `isVenditore` to `true`.
- Server sends a confirmation email to the user (if applicable).
- Client displays a success message: "You are now a registered seller."

Title: Add a new product (valid input)
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as a seller.
- User is on the "Add Product" page.
Test Data:
- Product Name: `Wireless Headphones`
- Price: `99.99`
- Description: `Noise-cancelling wireless headphones with 30-hour battery life.`
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Add Product" button.
2. User fills in the product details with the provided data.
3. User clicks the "Save" button.
Expected Result:
- Server validates all fields (non-empty, valid price format).
- Server creates a new product record in the database.
- Client displays a success message: "Product added successfully."

Title: Add a product with empty fields
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as a seller.
- User is on the "Add Product" page.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Add Product" button.
2. User clicks the "Save" button without filling any fields.
Expected Result:
- Server detects empty fields.
- Client displays error messages for each empty field:
- "Product name is required."
- "Price is required."
- "Description is required."
- Product is not added to the database.

Title: Modify an existing product (valid input)
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as a seller.
- A product exists with the following details:
- Name: `Wireless Headphones`
- Price: `99.99`
- Description: `Noise-cancelling wireless headphones with 30-hour battery life.`
Test Data:
- New Name: `Premium Wireless Headphones`
- New Price: `129.99`
- New Description: `Noise-cancelling wireless headphones with 40-hour battery life and built-in microphone.`
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the existing product.
2. User clicks the "Modify" button.
3. User updates the product details with the provided data.
4. User clicks the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result:
- Server validates all fields (non-empty, valid price format).
- Server updates the product record in the database.
- Client displays a success message: "Product updated successfully."

Title: Modify a product with invalid price format
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as a seller.
- A product exists with valid details.
Test Data:
- New Name: `Premium Wireless Headphones`
- New Price: `abc` (invalid)
- New Description: `Updated description.`
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the existing product.
2. User clicks the "Modify" button.
3. User updates the product details with the provided data.
4. User clicks the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result:
- Server detects invalid price format.
- Client highlights the price field in red and displays an error message: "Please enter a valid price."
- Product details are not updated in the database.

Title: Mark a seller as preferred
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as a client.
- A seller exists with a product.
Test Steps:
1. User views a product sold by a seller.
2. User clicks the "Mark as Preferred Seller" button.
3. User assigns a rating (e.g., 5 stars).
4. User confirms the action.
Expected Result:
- Server creates a `VenditorePreferito` record linking the client and seller.
- Server updates the seller's vote (e.g., `getVoto()` returns `5`).
- Client displays a success message: "Seller added to your preferences."

Title: View preferred sellers list
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as a client.
- User has marked at least one seller as preferred.
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the "Preferred Sellers" section.
Expected Result:
- Client displays a list of preferred sellers with their:
- Seller name.
- Voto (rating).
- Number of products sold (if available).
- User can click on a seller to view their products.

Title: Send push notification to user
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- User has enabled push notifications.
- A new product is added by a preferred seller.
Test Steps:
1. Server detects a new product from a preferred seller.
2. Server triggers the `push()` operation for the new product.
Expected Result:
- Server sends a push notification to the user's device.
- Client receives the notification with details:
- Title: "New product from your preferred seller!"
- Body: "Check out the latest product from [Seller Name]."

Title: Send in-app notification for new product
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- A new product is added by any seller.
Test Steps:
1. Server detects a new product.
2. Server triggers the `inAppNotification()` operation for the new product.
Expected Result:
- Client displays an in-app notification banner at the top of the screen.
- Notification content:
- Title: "New Product Available!"
- Body: "A new product has been added. Tap to view."
- User can dismiss the notification or tap to view the product.

Title: Disable push notifications
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Push notifications are currently enabled.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Settings" section.
2. User toggles the "Push Notifications" switch to `false`.
3. User clicks "Save."
Expected Result:
- Server updates the user's notification preferences.
- Client displays a confirmation message: "Push notifications disabled."
